text.title: CSS-in-JS Advanced Anti-patterns
text.summary: Performance and correctness failures in zero-runtime and runtime CSS-in-JS: render-time styled definitions, missing defaultVariants, Sprinkles size explosion, Emotion App Router setup.

antipattern.description: Defining styled.div or recipe() calls inside a React component render body.
antipattern.reason: Creates a new component class or stylesheet on every render. Move styled components and recipes to module scope; pass dynamic values via CSS variables or transient props.

antipattern.description: Using vars: { color: '#3b82f6' } in vanilla-extract instead of createVar() + assignVars.
antipattern.reason: Inline var assignment bypasses the typed contract; switching themes requires touching every .css.ts file instead of reassigning variables at the theme boundary.

antipattern.description: Omitting defaultVariants in a recipe() definition.
antipattern.reason: When a consumer calls button({ color: undefined }), vanilla-extract throws at runtime because no fallback exists for the undefined variant slot.

antipattern.description: Adding Emotion to Next.js App Router without the 'use client' CacheProvider wrapper.
antipattern.reason: Emotion runtime requires a client context; without the wrapper, the css prop silently degrades to a plain style attribute and FOUC occurs on first paint.

antipattern.description: Importing app-level modules (environment checks, API clients) from .css.ts files.
antipattern.reason: vanilla-extract runs .css.ts in a Node build sandbox; browser globals and async imports are unavailable and crash the build with cryptic "cannot find module" errors.

antipattern.description: Memoizing css template literal calls instead of lifting styled components to module scope.
antipattern.reason: Memoization reduces re-computation but the component class is still created inside the component function; only module-scope definitions produce stable class names.
